content:
{
  "title": "Counter-Adversarial Prompt Chaining: Dynamic Prompt Interleaving for Robust LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models are susceptible to adversarial prompts and jailbreak attempts that bypass safety constraints, often due to reliance on static, single-shot prompting. Existing prompting methods fail to dynamically adapt to adversarial tactics that emerge throughout a user-model interaction.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed in real-world, high-stakes applications, new jailbreak strategies rapidly appear. Ensuring model robustness is urgent, but current single-pass safety prompts struggle to anticipate or counter adaptive adversaries. There is a need for prompting frameworks that actively adapt to evolving adversarial inputs during a session.",
  "method": "We propose Counter-Adversarial Prompt Chaining: a dynamic prompting method where each user input triggers an interleaved generation of safety-reflective sub-prompts and constraint-checking probes, inserted probabilistically throughout the conversational context. These interleaved prompts are not static; they are conditioned on both prior user turns and the model’s own intermediate outputs, forming a recursive chain reaction that actively disrupts adversarial prompt composition. The method leverages retrieval-augmented safety exemplars and adversarial paraphrase detectors to synthesize targeted sub-prompts, making jailbreaks significantly harder and less predictable.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on AdvBench, JailbreakBench, and custom adversarial prompt datasets. Baselines include static safety prompting, reinforcement learning-based guardrails, and retrieval-augmented context injection. Metrics include attack success rate, model helpfulness retention, and prompt injection transferability. Human review will assess false positives and user experience impacts."
}
